pom.xml.general_idea: Update the target Java version property from 17 to 21 to align with the migration goal.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the build targets Java 17, failing to leverage Java 21 features and potentially causing runtime incompatibilities if Java 21-specific APIs are used.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated source/target compiler configuration with the modern release flag for Java 21 compatibility.
pom.xml.why_exists: The 'source' and 'target' options are deprecated in newer JDKs and may be removed; using 'release' ensures correct class file version and module path handling for Java 21.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add release flag to the test compiler configuration to ensure tests are compiled for Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this, tests might be compiled with default or legacy settings, potentially causing runtime errors or incompatibilities with Java 21 features in test code.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the code may fail to compile or produce warnings/errors depending on the compiler configuration (e.g., --enable-preview or strict serialization checks).

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct semantic marking of serialization metadata.
